phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean your way — short-range root bursts edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight edge at level 2: your short-range root bursts beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean your way — short-range root bursts edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your short-range root bursts beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — short-range root bursts edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your short-range root bursts on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Respect window: at two-plus items the wall-frost poke control beat your short-range root bursts cleanly. Trade only off Anivia's cooldowns and keep the wave where the lead can't cash out.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Your short-range root bursts out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control at level 1 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 your short-range root bursts beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Your short-range root bursts out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control at level 3 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 your short-range root bursts beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Your short-range root bursts out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control at the level-6 breakpoint — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Malignance spike: your R comes back absurdly fast and every tomb chains into the slow field. Force picks with E-W-R.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Liss opens every fight: claw in, tomb the carry, root the peel, and walk out through the panic.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-shatter the wave and chip his melee step-ups — your early Q harass is better than the lane gives it credit for.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: anyone who closes onto you eats a root. The anti-engage layer is already real at level 2.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean your way — short-range root bursts edge out the soldier-poke shuffle control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — short-range root bursts edge out the soldier-poke shuffle control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-shatter the wave and chip his melee step-ups — your early Q harass is better than the lane gives it credit for.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: anyone who closes onto you eats a root. The anti-engage layer is already real at level 2.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E claw completes the kit — you now have the lane's strangest engage and a guaranteed exit. Greed carefully; the claw is slow.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean his way — the grounded-zone DPS out-tick your short-range root bursts in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Frozen Tomb online — you are now the best anti-assassin and anti-dive mid in the game. Self-R turns every dive into a counter-kill.

phases.why: Malignance spike: your R comes back absurdly fast and every tomb chains into the slow field. Force picks with E-W-R.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items the numbers lean his way — the reflect-barrier poke out-tick your short-range root bursts in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-shatter the wave and chip his melee step-ups — your early Q harass is better than the lane gives it credit for.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: anyone who closes onto you eats a root. The anti-engage layer is already real at level 2.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E claw completes the kit — you now have the lane's strangest engage and a guaranteed exit. Greed carefully; the claw is slow.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your short-range root bursts beat Neeko's clone-root deception on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — short-range root bursts edge out the clone-root deception when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the double-blade spirit dives out-tick your short-range root bursts in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Yone's double-blade spirit dives own this window — your short-range root bursts don't answer them at two-plus items yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-shatter the wave and chip his melee step-ups — your early Q harass is better than the lane gives it credit for.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: anyone who closes onto you eats a root. The anti-engage layer is already real at level 2.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E claw completes the kit — you now have the lane's strangest engage and a guaranteed exit. Greed carefully; the claw is slow.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — Q spam stops hurting your mana. Chip Locke every time he touches the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Frozen Tomb online — you are now the best anti-assassin and anti-dive mid in the game. Self-R turns every dive into a counter-kill.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Malignance spike: your R comes back absurdly fast and every tomb chains into the slow field. Force picks with E-W-R.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Liss opens every fight: claw in, tomb the carry, root the peel, and walk out through the panic.
